"   ACTVT: 03 (display)
" Checked by: SAP kernel during SELECT from VBAK
" Error: user sees only their org's orders (automatic)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle OData data type mapping when writing TS from FS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS fields ke liye correct OData data type choose karna important hai — wrong type se Fiori UI issues aate hain. String fields: Edm.String with MaxLength (VBELN MaxLength=10, KUNAG MaxLength=10). Amount fields: Edm.Decimal with Precision aur Scale (NETWR Precision=15 Scale=2). Date fields: Edm.DateTime (ERDAT). Boolean: Edm.Boolean. Integer: Edm.Int32. ABAP DDIC type se OData type automatically import ho jaata hai agar SEGW mein 'Import from DDIC' option use karo — recommended approach.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When writing TS, I map ABAP DDIC field types to OData EDM types. String (CHAR, NUMC): Edm.String with MaxLength matching the ABAP length. Amount (CURR, DEC with currency): Edm.Decimal with Precision and Scale. Date (DATS): Edm.DateTime. Boolean (CHAR 1 with X/space): Edm.Boolean. Quantity (QUAN): Edm.Decimal. I document the currency key field when mapping amount fields — OData requires a separate Currency property associated with Edm.Decimal amounts for proper Fiori formatting. In SEGW, I use 'Import ABAP Structure' to auto-map types and then adjust where needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Amount fields without associated currency key field cause Fiori formatting issues — amounts display without currency symbol or in wrong format. Always map amount fields with their currency key (WAERK for VBAK amounts).
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Mapping DATS (date) to Edm.String — dates display as '20240101' string in Fiori instead of a proper date. Always use Edm.DateTime for ABAP DATS fields.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS data type mapping table:
" ABAP Field   DDIC Type   OData Type          Notes
" VBELN        CHAR(10)    Edm.String(10)      Order number
" NETWR        CURR(13,2)  Edm.Decimal(15,2)   Amount
" WAERK        CUKY(5)     Edm.String(5)       Currency key
" ERDAT        DATS(8)     Edm.DateTime        Order date
" GBSTK        CHAR(1)     Edm.String(1)       Status char
" KWMENG       QUAN(13,3)  Edm.Decimal(13,3)   Quantity

" SEGW: Import from ABAP Structure for auto-mapping
" Entity Type → Properties → Import → ABAP Structure

followupAnswerBank.question: What questions should you ask the functional consultant when you receive a vague FS for OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Vague FS common hai — 'Show sales orders' bolta hai but kaunse fields? Kaunse filters? Kaunse users? Clarification questions: 1) Kaunse fields Fiori screen pe dikhne chahiye? 2) Filters mandatory hain ya optional? 3) Kaunse user roles access karenge? 4) Performance expectation kya hai — kitne records ek baar mein? 5) Navigation chahiye (line items, documents)? 6) Create/Update bhi chahiye ya sirf Read? 7) Mobile device pe bhi chalega? Ye questions puchke TS complete banaao.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When FS is vague, I ask the functional consultant: Which fields are required on screen versus nice-to-have (drives entity properties list)? Which filters are mandatory versus optional (drives filter validation in DPC_EXT)? What is the expected data volume per user session (drives $top limits and mandatory filter decisions)? Do we need navigation to related documents like line items or deliveries (drives association design)? Are create or update operations required (drives which DPC_EXT methods to implement)? What authorization restrictions apply by role (drives auth object selection)? These answers transform a vague FS into a precise TS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS review meeting is an investment — 1 hour clarifying before development saves 5-10 hours of rework. Make it a standard practice: 'I will write the TS draft after this meeting and share for your review before coding starts.'
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming answers to unclear FS questions and coding based on assumptions — classic cause of rework. Always clarify ambiguities before TS is finalized.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS review checklist (before writing TS):
" [ ] Field list confirmed (entity properties)
" [ ] Mandatory vs optional filters confirmed
" [ ] Data volume estimate received
" [ ] Navigation requirements (related entities) confirmed
" [ ] CRUD operations scope confirmed (read-only vs CRUD)
" [ ] User roles and auth restrictions confirmed
" [ ] Device requirements (desktop/mobile/tablet)
" [ ] Error message requirements documented
" [ ] UAT data availability confirmed

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you write the entity key definition when converting FS to TS for OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Key definition critical hai — wrong key se GET_ENTITY fail hoga aur $expand bhi broken hoga. Single key entity: SalesOrder (VBELN) — ek field key hai. Composite key entity: SalesOrderItem (VBELN + POSNR) — do fields milke key banate hain. SEGW mein key fields property pe 'Is Key' checkbox mark karo. Key fields OData URL mein aate hain: single key → /SalesOrderSet('0000012345'), composite key → /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010'). FS mein 'unique identifier' kya hai — woh key banta hai. Business rules: SAP document numbers naturally unique hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: From the FS, I identify what uniquely identifies each business object. For SalesOrder, VBELN (10-char) is the single key. For SalesOrderItem, the combination of VBELN and POSNR forms the composite key. In the TS I document: entity name, key field names, data types, and MaxLength. In SEGW, I mark these properties as keys. For composite keys, the OData URL uses named key predicates: /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010'). Wrong key definition causes GET_ENTITY failures and incorrect navigation — always verify key with the functional consultant and confirm with SE11 table primary key.

followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SEGW generates key-based URL patterns automatically from your key definitions. Getting keys wrong early means changing them later breaks generated code — costly rework. Nail the key design in TS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using wrong key fields — for example using internal ID instead of the business document number displayed to users. Confirm key fields with functional consultant: 'Is this the number users will use to identify the record?'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS key definitions:
" Entity: SalesOrder
" Key: SalesOrder VBELN Edm.String MaxLength=10 IsKey=true
" URL: /SalesOrderSet('0000012345')

" Entity: SalesOrderItem (composite key)
" Keys: SalesOrder VBELN Edm.String MaxLength=10 IsKey=true
"       ItemNumber  POSNR Edm.String MaxLength=6  IsKey=true
" URL: /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010')

" SE11 verify: primary key of VBAK = MANDT + VBELN
" (MANDT excluded from OData — client always current)
